title: Input Reading, Tokenization, Command Registry, and Dispatch · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent conservation group receives a different region, command sequence, API fixture, cache age, concurrency budget, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers input reading, tokenization, command registry, and dispatch into a changed deterministic command parser and injected dispatcher. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of deterministic command parser and injected dispatcher with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Choose bufio.Reader or Scanner from line-size, token, error, cancellation, and testability requirements and configure any explicit limit.

Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Pokedex Go Reader Choice
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 1511 during a disconnected community-lab recovery drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile line admission, argument validation, registry lookup, and deterministic dispatch; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use traffic budget must remain enforced; then change one command, argument, EOF, or output-width condition and preserve the session contract. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose bufio.Reader or Scanner from line-size, token, error, cancellation, and testability requirements and configure any explicit limit.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Pokedex Go Dispatch Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-go-dispatch-evidence-1ma1eg" that uses case 8005 to reconcile line admission, argument validation, registry lookup, and deterministic dispatch. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.
